blocks.content:
**What you'll learn:** A quick tour of what’s new and better in Grafana 13. It all runs on Learn, so you don’t need your own stack to follow along.

Each stop jumps you to a real example, where you can open it, click around, and see how the feature behaves in an actual UI.

**Suggested dashboards** is a feature designed to reduce the time it takes for you to create your first effective dashboard. Suggested dashboards address the challenge of an empty dashboard by leveraging the context of your data connections. The empty dashboard now includes suggestions for pre-built dashboards based on your connected data sources. 

With these dashboards, curated from both Grafana and the Community, you can achieve faster initial time-to-insight.
**Data sources** — Navigate to the data sources page, then follow the interactive steps below to try the new and improved visualization suggestions.
Click **Build a dashboard** to start seeing suggested dashboards for this data source
You should now see some suggested dashboards based on the Prometheus data source!
See what the community has built to get more insights!

steps.description: Click **View all** to see all suggested dashboards from both Grafana and the Community
Click the **Community** tab to see all Prometheus suggested dashboards built by the community.

blocks.content:
You can now create dashboards from pre-defined Grafana templates! 🙌 

Grafana provides a variety of pre-built dashboard templates that you can use to quickly set up visualizations for your data. These dashboards use sample data, which you can replace with your own data, making it easier to get started with monitoring and analysis.
**Dashboards** — Navigate to the dashboards page, then follow the interactive steps below to try and create a dashboard from a template.
Follow along to learn how to create a dashboard from a template

steps.description: Select **Dashboard from template** in the dropdown menu
You should now see pre-defined templates that you can start using instantly
